stytch.name: Stytch
stytch.short_description: Stytch is an identity platform providing authentication, authorization, and fraud prevention APIs for humans and AI agents.
stytch.starting_price: $0/month (Pay As You Go, free up to 10,000 MAUs)

features.description: SAML and OIDC single sign-on plus SCIM provisioning, with the first 5 SSO/SCIM connections included free.
features.title: Enterprise SSO & SCIM

features.description: Passkeys, magic links, and one-time passcodes delivered via SMS and email, with automatic provider failover for delivery reliability.
features.title: Passwordless authentication

features.description: Authentication for service-to-service calls and AI agents, with 1,000 M2M tokens included in the free tier.
features.title: Machine-to-machine (M2M) authentication

features.description: Device fingerprinting and intelligent rate limiting, described as achieving 99.99% bot detection accuracy, with 10,000 free fingerprints per month.
features.title: Fraud and bot protection

features.description: Organization-level policies, RBAC, and SCIM support for building B2B applications with unlimited organizations, even on the free tier.
features.title: Turnkey multi-tenancy

features.description: A self-service portal that lets enterprise customers manage their own SSO and SCIM connections.
features.title: Embeddable Admin Portal

features.description: Support for cross-application integrations between a company's own products and third-party apps.
features.title: Connected Apps

features.description: Prebuilt, customizable login UI components alongside headless SDKs and backend APIs for full flexibility.
features.title: Web & mobile UI components

pros: Free tier includes 10,000 monthly active users/AI agents, unlimited organizations, 5 SSO/SCIM connections, and 1,000 M2M tokens
Pricing has no hard caps or pricing cliffs, so overages are billed for actual usage only
Explicit support for machine-to-machine and AI agent authentication, not just human end users
Broad auth method coverage including passkeys, OAuth, SSO, sessions, and RBAC in the base tier
Backed by Twilio after Stytch joined the company, adding messaging infrastructure depth
cons: SSO/SCIM connections beyond the free 5 cost $125 per connection, similar to competitors
Enterprise pricing, including HIPAA/BAA compliance and a 99.99% uptime SLA, is custom and not publicly listed
Brand customization requires a separate $99 one-time fee
Fraud prevention fingerprinting beyond the free 10,000 is billed per use at $0.005 each

faqs.answer: Yes. The Pay As You Go plan is free up to 10,000 monthly active users/AI agents, 5 SSO or SCIM connections, and 1,000 M2M tokens.
faqs.question: Is Stytch free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes, Stytch positions itself as an identity platform for both humans and AI agents, and its free tier explicitly covers AI agents alongside human monthly active users.
faqs.question: Does Stytch support authentication for AI agents?

faqs.answer: Stytch charges usage-based overages with no hard caps or pricing cliffs, plus $125 per additional SSO/SCIM connection beyond the 5 included free.
faqs.question: What happens if usage exceeds the free tier?

faqs.answer: Yes, Stytch has joined Twilio to build what the company describes as the intelligent identity layer for the internet.
faqs.question: Is Stytch part of Twilio?

faqs.answer: Yes, via device fingerprinting (10,000 free per month, then $0.005 each) and intelligent rate limiting for bot detection.
faqs.question: Does Stytch offer fraud protection?

Desktop apps for Windows, macOS, and Linux plus mobile apps for Android and iOS

Advanced features like AI summaries and a High Performance Backend for large calls require enterprise tiers
Requires self-managed Nextcloud infrastructure unless using a third-party Nextcloud hosting provider

faqs.answer: Yes, Nextcloud Talk is open source and free to self-host as part of Nextcloud; paid Enterprise Talk plans add extended support and scaling features.

faqs.answer: Yes, it supports end-to-end encrypted calls and federated chat/video.

faqs.answer: It's available on the web, desktop apps for Windows, macOS, and Linux, and mobile apps for Android and iOS.
faqs.question: What platforms does Nextcloud Talk support?

faqs.answer: Yes, it supports on-premises and air-gapped deployments.
faqs.question: Can Nextcloud Talk run without internet access?

faqs.answer: Published Enterprise Talk pricing starts at €42/user/year for 100 users, dropping to €31.50/user/year at 250 users.

Scaling to large deployments requires configuring worker processes rather than working out of the box

Large community per vendor claims: 64k+ GitHub stars and 20M+ Docker downloads
Auto-generated REST APIs and a built-in App Store of integrations
Both cloud-hosted and self-hosted deployment options, with a genuine free cloud tier
cons: The self-hosted Community Edition moved from AGPLv3 to the Sustainable Use License, a source-available license rather than an OSI-approved open-source license, which drew criticism from parts of the self-hosting community
Cloud free plan is capped at 1,000 records and 1GB storage
SSO, audit logs, and row-level security are reserved for Business/Scale/Enterprise tiers
The Scale plan requires a minimum of 3 seats

faqs.answer: Yes. NocoDB offers a free cloud plan (3 editor seats, 1,000 records) and a free, self-hosted Community Edition.
faqs.question: Is NocoDB free?

faqs.answer: The self-hosted Community Edition is distributed under the Sustainable Use License, a source-available license, after the project moved away from AGPLv3.
faqs.question: What license does NocoDB use?

faqs.answer: Yes, NocoDB can turn an existing Postgres or MySQL database into a spreadsheet-style interface rather than requiring data import.
faqs.question: Can NocoDB connect to an existing database?

faqs.answer: Yes, every base gets auto-generated REST APIs, webhooks, and an SDK.
faqs.question: Does NocoDB provide an API?

faqs.answer: Yes, via Docker, binary installers for macOS/Linux/Windows, or an install script.
faqs.question: Is NocoDB available self-hosted?

baserow.name: Baserow
baserow.short_description: Baserow is an open-source, self-hostable no-code database and application builder positioned as an Airtable alternative.

features.description: Structure and view data through Grid, Gallery, Kanban, Calendar, Timeline, Form, and Survey views.
features.title: Database Builder

features.description: Build front-end apps and portals on top of Baserow data without code, publishable to a custom domain.
features.title: Application Builder

features.description: Workflow automations with triggers, branches, conditions, and loops.
features.title: Automation Builder

features.description: Chart-based dashboards for visualizing data stored in Baserow.
features.title: Dashboards

features.description: Builds data structures and automations from natural-language descriptions, available on paid plans.
features.title: Kuma AI assistant

features.description: Native HTTP, email, and webhook support, plus connectors for Zapier, Make, and n8n.
features.title: Integrations

features.description: Cloud-hosted, or self-hosted via Docker, Docker Compose, Helm, Cloudron, Railway, Elestio, Heroku, DigitalOcean, or AWS.
features.title: Flexible hosting

features.description: The cloud offering is GDPR, HIPAA, and SOC 2 Type II compliant.
features.title: Enterprise compliance

pros: MIT-licensed open-source core with a genuine self-hosting option
Combines database, application, automation, and dashboard builders in one platform
Cloud version is GDPR, HIPAA, and SOC 2 Type II compliant
65+ templates included on the free plan
Multiple self-hosting paths, including one-click deploys on Cloudron, Railway, Elestio, and Heroku
cons: Free plan is capped at 3,000 rows per workspace and 2GB storage
Kuma AI assistant and other premium features require a paid plan
Audit logs and custom roles are limited to Advanced/Enterprise tiers
Smaller open-source community (about 5,400 GitHub stars) than some rival no-code database tools

faqs.answer: Yes, Baserow's core is released under the MIT license; premium and enterprise features are offered as a source-available add-on under an open-core model.
faqs.question: Is Baserow open source?

faqs.answer: Yes, via Docker, Docker Compose, Helm, or hosted providers like Cloudron, Railway, Elestio, Heroku, Render, DigitalOcean, and AWS.
faqs.question: Can Baserow be self-hosted?

faqs.answer: Yes. The Free plan includes 3,000 rows per workspace, 2GB storage, and 65+ templates.
faqs.question: Does Baserow have a free plan?

faqs.answer: According to Baserow's website, the cloud-hosted version is GDPR, HIPAA, and SOC 2 Type II compliant.
faqs.question: Is Baserow HIPAA and SOC 2 compliant?

faqs.answer: Yes, its Kuma AI assistant can generate data structures and automations from natural-language descriptions, starting on the Premium plan.
faqs.question: Does Baserow include AI features?
